item.title: Explainer-When are Turkey's next elections, and can Erdogan run again?
item.description:
By Huseyin Hayatsever

ANKARA, July 21 (Reuters) - Turkey's opposition leader, Ozgur Ozel, said he would form a new party to challenge Tayyip Erdogan, a political splintering that could boost the president's prospects of extending his 23-year rule at the next elections.

item.title: 'Interaction' with Iran's injured Supreme Leader increasing daily, president says
item.description:
DUBAI, July 21 (Reuters) - The level of "interaction" with Iran's Supreme Leader is increasing day by day, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said in a video clip published on state media on Tuesday.

His remarks follow months of speculation about the health of Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, who has not been seen in public since the airstrike that killed his father and predecessor, Ali Khamenei, at the start of the Iran war.

item.title: Turkey's opposition leader to form new party to challenge Erdogan
item.description:
By Huseyin Hayatsever

ANKARA, July 21 (Reuters) - Turkish opposition leader Ozgur Ozel said on Tuesday he would resign his post and form a new party, a political splintering that compounds the challenges facing President Tayyip Erdogan's opponents as they face an unprecedented legal crackdown.

The new party is expected to spark an exodus of voters and parliamentarians from the secularist Republican People's Party (CHP), which modern Turkey's founder Mustafa Kemal Ataturk established more than a century ago.

item.title: Houthis warn shipping firms to avoid Saudi ports or face attack: What to know
item.description: The Houthi militia reportedly emailed multiple shipping companies on Tuesday, warning them of the consequences of visiting Saudi ports.

item.title: Houthis warn shipping firms to avoid Saudi ports or face attack: What to know
item.description: The Houthi militia reportedly emailed multiple shipping companies on Tuesday, warning them of the consequences of visiting Saudi ports.

item.title: Houthis warn shipping firms to avoid Saudi ports or face attack: What to know
item.description: The Houthi militia reportedly emailed multiple shipping companies on Tuesday, warning them of the consequences of visiting Saudi ports.

item.title: Houthis warn shipping firms to avoid Saudi ports or face attack: What to know
item.description: The Houthi militia reportedly emailed multiple shipping companies on Tuesday, warning them of the consequences of visiting Saudi ports.

item.title: Kuwaitis ration electricity as Iran hits power grid
item.description: Residents of Kuwait, one of the world's hottest countries, are cutting their use of air conditioning at the height of summer, as Iranian strikes on power plants turn rationing into a national priority. Energy-guzzling air conditioners are ubiquitous in Kuwait during the summer months.

item.title: Houthis warn shipping companies to avoid Saudi ports, email shows
item.description:
By Jonathan Saul and Renee Maltezou

LONDON/ATHENS, July 21 (Reuters) - Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthi militia told shipping companies not to load or discharge cargo at Saudi Arabian ports or they may be targeted, according to an email the group sent to companies.

The Houthis on Monday declared a naval blockade against Saudi Arabia, opening a potential new front against the United States in its war with Iran and raising the threat to global energy supplies and trade beyond the Gulf.

item.title: Iran steps up attacks in return to war with US
item.description: Iran stepped up its attacks in the Middle East on Tuesday, two weeks after its war with the United States resumed, with sirens sounding in Bahrain and Jordan announcing it had intercepted two waves of missiles and drones. "With this radar sweep, the enemy should prepare for even more decisive and powerful waves of drone and missile attacks," the Guards said in a statement carried by state news agency IRNA. Following the deaths of at least three US soldiers in the past several days, the United States launched a new round of attacks against Iran on Monday which it said were "designed to further degrade Iranian military capabilities used to attack commercial shipping" in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Saudi crude tankers turn back as Houthis open new front in US-Iran war
item.description:
By Jana Choukeir, Nayera Abdallah and Elwely Elwelly

DUBAI, July 21 (Reuters) - Two oil tankers carrying Saudi crude to Asia reversed course in the Red Sea on Tuesday after threats from Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis, as a widening Middle East conflict disrupted shipping through two of the world's most critical energy chokepoints.

item.title: Global hunger declines across continents but climate, trade risks loom, UN says
item.description:
By Giselda Vagnoni and Oriana Boselli

ROME, July 21 (Reuters) - Global hunger declined for a third consecutive year in 2025, with improvements recorded across every continent, although mounting risks from climate shocks and trade disruption could threaten further progress, a United Nations report said.

Around 645 million people, or 7.8% of the world's population, experienced hunger last year, down from 8.1% in 2024 and 8.6% in 2022, according to the State of Food Security and Nutrition in the World report, jointly prepared by five U.N. agencies.

item.title: Israeli airstrike kills family of six in Gaza, medics say
item.description:
By Nidal al-Mughrabi and Dawoud Abu Alkas

CAIRO/GAZA, July 21 (Reuters) - An Israeli airstrike on Tuesday killed a father, his wife and their four children in Gaza City, while setting their home ablaze, health officials said, in the latest deadly attack since a U.S.-backed ceasefire was agreed more than nine months ago.

Medics said the Israeli strike on the family house in the Sabra neighbourhood killed the man, Firas Al-Masri, his wife, Salsabeel, and their three daughters and son.

item.title: Lebanese army enters southern town after Israeli withdrawal, in first test of US-brokered deal
item.description:
By Maya Gebeily

BEIRUT, July 21 (Reuters) - Lebanese army troops began deploying in the southern Lebanese town of Zawtar al-Gharbiyeh on Tuesday after Israeli forces withdrew from the area, a senior Lebanese security official said, in line with a U.S.-brokered plan.

The plan foresees Lebanese army troops confiscating weapons from the Iran-aligned Hezbollah group in parts of southern Lebanon and phased withdrawals of Israeli troops. The Israeli military said on Monday that the plan, dubbed the pilot zone programme, has begun. It had no immediate comment on Tuesday.

item.title: In Gaza, a mother's hunt for water lays bare a worsening health crisis
item.description:
By Nidal al-Mughrabi and Dawoud Abu Alkas

CAIRO/GAZA, July 21 (Reuters) - In pre-dawn darkness, Ibtisam Al-Helou leaves her tent on Gaza's seafront on a daily search for fresh water. The Mediterranean lies only a few steps away, but the water she needs to cook, drink and wash will mean a trek across Gaza City.

Setting out before the heat rises to the scorching 33 degrees Celsius (91 F) of midday, the 40-year-old mother of three makes her way through shattered neighbourhoods where word has spread that water might be available.

item.title: Lebanon's Aoun to present Trump with Hezbollah disarmament plan as army deploys in south
item.description:
By Nandita Bose, Maya Gebeily and Tom Perry

WASHINGTON/BEIRUT, July 21 (Reuters) - Lebanese President Joseph Aoun met U.S. President Donald Trump at the White House on Tuesday to present a plan on how to disarm Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah and secure Israel's withdrawal from Lebanon.

Aoun, who served as the commander of Lebanon's U.S.-backed army before being elected president last year, is the first Lebanese head of state in nearly 20 years to visit the White House. He arrived Tuesday morning and met Trump face-to-face for the first time.

item.title: US military completes its latest strikes on Iran, marking the 10th successive night of attacks
item.description:
By Kanishka Singh

WASHINGTON, July 20 (Reuters) - The U.S. military said late on Monday it finished its latest round of strikes on Iran, saying it hit Iranian military command centers, missile and drone launch sites, maritime capabilities and air defense systems.

The U.S. Central Command made the announcement in a post on social media website X.

• The latest round of U.S. strikes on Iran lasted about five hours, according to the U.S. military, marking 10 consecutive nights of American strikes on Iran.

item.title: 'All these rashes': chickenpox surges in Gaza camps
item.description: Eight-year-old Ruba Saadeh spends her days confined to the tent she shares with her family in Gaza, her body dotted with inflamed red blisters as chickenpox sweeps through overcrowded camps like the one they live in. he said, as he diagnosed a child who had red blisters on his stomach.

item.title: Lebanon's Aoun to meet Trump as pressure builds to disarm Hezbollah
item.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun was to meet with his US counterpart Donald Trump in Washington on Tuesday, as Beirut's military moved into an area of the south under a deal with Israel to disarm Hezbollah. Aoun is the first Lebanese president to be invited to Washington since 2009.

item.title: ASEAN top diplomats meet, voice 'serious concern' over Middle East hostilities
item.description:
By Karen Lema and Nestor Corrales

MANILA, July 21 (Reuters) - The Middle East crisis overshadowed a diplomatic gathering in Southeast Asia on Tuesday, where foreign ministers voiced concerns over renewed hostilities that add to uncertainty in a region grappling to manage tensions and conflicts of its own.
